page: Microsoft Azure F48as_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Fasv6 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable. The hypervisor utilized is Microsoft Hyper-V.

In benchmark evaluations, the F48as_v6 demonstrates top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as cached read-only small block memory bandwidth. Multi-threaded operations, such as bzip3 decompression and software compilation with Clang, also rank in the top 10%. Large block memory bandwidth and Passmark CPU scores fall in the top 25%. For language model workloads, the server achieves top 10% prompt processing speeds with a small 135M model, though performance drops to average when processing prompts and generating text with a large 70B model.

The F48as_v6 offers high compute density with dedicated physical cores and a 3.7 GHz clock speed, making it highly efficient for CPU-bound workloads. However, the lack of local storage requires reliance on external Premium Storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs compared to local NVMe options. The 4.0 GB of RAM per core provides a balanced resource profile, but workloads requiring massive memory-to-CPU ratios may find the memory capacity limiting.

This server configuration is suited for multi-threaded CPU tasks, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations. It is also capable of handling small to medium-sized LLM inference tasks, though it is not optimized for large-scale model generation due to the absence of dedicated GPU hardware.
description: The Microsoft Azure F48as_v6 is an x86_64 server from the Fasv6 family, powered by a dedicated 48-core AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core) and lacks local storage, relying instead on Premium Storage compatibility. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the server delivers top-tier performance in stress-ng single-core and multi-core benchmarks, as well as multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, static web serving, and small-to-medium LLM inference. However, its performance scales down to average on large 70B LLM tasks, and the absence of local NVMe storage or dedicated GPUs represents a key architectural tradeoff. This configuration is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads, software development pipelines, and web serving applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F48as_v6 is a dedicated 48-core AMD EPYC server with 192 GB RAM. It delivers top-tier CPU and multi-threaded performance for compute-intensive workloads and software compilation.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F48as_v6 is a 48-core AMD EPYC server with 192 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance.
tagline: A dedicated forty-eight core AMD EPYC processor instance delivering top-tier compute performance and balanced memory for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated 48-core AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier performance in multi-core CPU benchmarks and software compilation
Premium Storage capable with no local storage included
Suited for multi-threaded compute, database operations, and web serving
